authoritative_sources.title: Project management plan - Full or Condensed Version
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA is a primary public owner for architecture and engineering delivery. This PMIS guidance explicitly defines PM plan signatories, milestone plans, risk registers, change management plans, and closeout documentation used in capital-project change handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Change intake, baseline framing, and approval packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Request for information
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA PMIS guidance is authoritative for owner-side A and E RFI workflow. It defines how RFIs are created, assigned, clarified, tracked, and closed, which materially changes change-package readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Clarification, dependency collection, and response routing

authoritative_sources.title: Use our project schedule management for small projects
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official GSA scheduling guidance describes baseline schedules, critical milestone logic, update discipline, and schedule issue handling used in public A and E project controls.

authoritative_sources.title: Develop and manage costs for RWA projects
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's RWA cost guidance defines estimate classes, estimate update posture, and change-order proposal evaluation for architecture and engineering work, making it directly relevant to estimate and approval packaging.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Estimate review and funding posture assessment

authoritative_sources.title: RWA process
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's RWA process guidance is authoritative for when revised SCEs, amended RWAs, new RWAs, beneficial occupancy, and closeout become necessary because of scope, cost, or schedule changes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Scope-change governance and closeout dependency review

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Manual, Section 5-3: Change Orders
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Caltrans is a major public infrastructure owner. Its official change-order manual covers classifications, approvals, funds status, time extensions, and project-engineer or senior concurrence boundaries that materially govern engineering change planning.

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Manual, Section 5-1: Project Records and Reports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Caltrans defines project-history files, estimate and project status records, FalconDMS, eRETA, and change-order record categories that anchor evidence traceability and systems-of-record expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance - Project Management Plans - Major Projects
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FHWA is an authoritative federal project-oversight body. Its PMP guidance defines scope, cost, schedule, document-control, change-management, and approval structures used in capital-project controls.

authoritative_sources.title: Contract Changes - Construction Program Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FHWA's contract-changes guide is authoritative for federal-aid construction contract modifications, changed-conditions procedures, and equitable-adjustment expectations that constrain change-package recommendations.

authoritative_sources.title: Project Requirements: Project Sign-Off and Completion
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NYC DOB is an authority having jurisdiction. Its sign-off and completion guidance defines permit sign-off, special inspections, objection resolution, DOB NOW handling, and completion prerequisites that the lane must treat as hard boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Permit, inspection, sign-off, and closeout gating

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and change-frame normalization
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request, confirm that it is architecture and engineering change work, and normalize RFI, design revision, estimate, funding, schedule, permit, and closeout context into one planning frame.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and system-of-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the current PM plan, RFI status, project-history record, drawing revision, estimate basis, and PMIS or AHJ record of truth before any sequence or resource recommendation is produced.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess staffing, review calendars, milestone logic, estimate completeness, site-readiness, permit windows, and inspection dependencies to determine whether the change is schedulable.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Estimate, schedule, and approval package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Prepare recommendation-only sequencing, deferral, funding-impact, and approval-needed outputs with named evidence, assumptions, and tradeoffs instead of claiming final authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Refresh, closeout, and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Withdraw or revise tentative recommendations when field, permit, inspection, funding, or closeout facts change and route to the licensed, permitting, contracting, or acceptance owner when authority leaves the lane.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: project management plans, change management plans, milestone plans, risk registers, signatory lists, and communications plans
RFI logs, clarification requests, design bulletins, drawing and specification revisions, and document-control history
pending change-item logs, change-order memoranda, revised SCE or IGE or ROM estimate packages, funding amendments, and pricing assumptions
baseline CPM or master schedules, schedule updates, look-aheads, recovery plans, staffing loads, and time-impact analyses
PMIS workflows, project-history files, FalconDMS records, eRETA records, permit trackers, AHJ portal status, and inspection records
punch lists, substantial-completion milestones, beneficial-occupancy caveats, as-builts, O and M deliverables, training records, and closeout packages
constraint logs, approval-needed summaries, project-controls summaries, change-readiness summaries, and escalation notes
systems_of_record: project management information system such as Kahua or tenant equivalent
document control or common data environment
estimating and funding systems for SCE, IGE, or change-pricing packages
scheduling system for CPM or master schedule baselines and updates
project-history repositories such as FalconDMS and eRETA where applicable
permit and AHJ portals such as DOB NOW where applicable

allowed: Classify A and E change-management work as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, systems-of-record links, and authoritative records required to continue safely.
Produce evidence-backed schedule, capacity, and allocation recommendations, project-controls summaries, and approval-needed packages without claiming final authority.
Map assumptions, dependencies, blockers, and adjacent-specialist routing for estimate, permit, site, and closeout impacts.
escalate: The source-of-truth RFI, drawing revision, estimate basis, permit status, inspection state, or closeout record is missing or materially conflicts across systems.
The request requires final design approval, engineer-of-record judgment, code interpretation, permit action, contracting authority, budget approval, or completion signoff.
Public guidance, tenant policy, contract posture, or jurisdictional requirements materially conflict on the next step.
A site-readiness, safety, inspection, or closeout condition changes whether work may proceed.
refuse: Approve a change order value, revised funding authority, permit filing, sealed design change, field execution, occupancy, or project completion.
Treat generic project-management or organizational-change inputs as sufficient substitutes for named A and E records such as RFIs, revisions, estimates, permits, site evidence, or closeout packages.
Advance a change package without traceable record-of-truth support for scope, cost, schedule, and approval ownership.

domain_failure_modes: sequencing a project change without the current RFI response, drawing revision, or estimate basis and sending the team into review on stale information
recommending a change path that ignores permit or inspection blockers and creates an unsafe or noncompliant site-readiness assumption
treating scope and funding shifts as planning-only issues when amended RWAs, revised SCEs, or contract-change procedures are actually required
using the wrong PMIS or project-history record and losing evidence continuity across change logs, approvals, and closeout impacts
allowing schedule pressure to imply engineer-of-record, code, permit, contracting, or acceptance authority that belongs elsewhere
declaring a late change manageable without reopening punch-list, as-built, training, occupancy, or closeout dependencies
collapsing the lane into generic change management and ignoring RFIs, change orders, project-history files, permit status, and closeout artifacts that make A and E delivery distinct

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Design-bid-build, CMAR, design-build, and owner-direct A and E delivery models shift approval paths, artifact names, and who owns cost or schedule commitments. Tenant runtime context must declare the contract model.

Public sources name Kahua, FalconDMS, eRETA, and DOB NOW, but tenants may use different systems and state models. Runtime retrieval wiring must map tenant systems to the public-baseline artifact expectations.

AHJ portals, permit amendments, inspection cadence, and sign-off prerequisites vary materially by jurisdiction. The pack preserves the public baseline and requires tenant or jurisdiction inputs where local rules change the answer.
